mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals stronger labor market dynamics, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1% signals stronger manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in both payrolls (PAYEMS) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS/RSAFS growth and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% signals economic contraction, prompting central bank intervention that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 70%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2% signals inflationary pressure, prompting tighter monetary policy that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL rises above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 units signals weak consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 1,000.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 6% and decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100k signals economic distress, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of UNRATE/PAYEMS fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling IC below 0.10.
